title: unable to combine options flags
body:
I was trying to use the equivalent of `podman rm $(podman ps -aq)` with `buildah`, but got an error when trying to combine the `-a` and `-q` options:

Using `buildah` 1.2 on Fedora 28 Silverblue:

```bash
$ buildah --version
buildah version 1.2 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)

$ sudo buildah containers -aq                                        
Incorrect Usage: flag provided but not defined: -aq                 
                                                  
NAME:                                                    
   buildah containers - List working containers and their base images
                                                   
USAGE:
   buildah containers [command options]
                      
DESCRIPTION:
   Lists containers which appear to be buildah working containers, their
   names and IDs, and the names and IDs of the images from which they were
   initialized

OPTIONS:
   --all, -a                 also list non-buildah containers
   --filter value, -f value  filter output based on conditions provided
   --format value            pretty-print containers using a Go template
   --json                    output in JSON format
   --noheading, -n           do not print column headings
   --notruncate              do not truncate output
   --quiet, -q               display only container IDs

flag provided but not defined: -aq

$ sudo buildah containers -a -q
b217b301a355fffa2d45a0d6a48507a067904928a6702d6041c84178a397abbe
e9f4d5cdd5d2a29a3d51bd8d190543ed96c71c8b54c33fde8c4fbe665bc11c1b

627f4d9119973f87f78ae6d2d9795b4e14b4ef34ea23e17b61d4d59809c1150e
f955bcdf63f38ab87673061c8a5be2db2fb84a533158910f2e8b7c847853d23f
45f1a0b81031b87b9760cf082c44744aa48b17a5d5d24d28b8ce3ab415e7c7ea
b445ccb00641eec49117f46ea0a8d8b5457600b614f6379bb6ea058ad4f47ab5

$ sudo buildah images -aq                  
Incorrect Usage: flag provided but not defined: -aq             
                                                                
NAME:                                                           
   buildah images - List images in local storage                
                                                                
USAGE:                                                          
   buildah images [command options] [imageName]                 
                                                                
DESCRIPTION:                                                    
   Lists locally stored images.                                 
                                                                
OPTIONS:                                                        
   --all, -a                 show all images, including intermediate images from a build
   --digests                 show digests
   --filter value, -f value  filter output based on conditions provided
   --format value            pretty-print images using a Go template
   --json                    output in JSON format
   --noheading, -n           do not print column headings
   --no-trunc, --notruncate  do not truncate output
   --quiet, -q               display only image IDs

flag provided but not defined: -aq

$ sudo buildah images -a -q
8ac48589692a53a9b8c2d1ceaa6b402665aa7fe667ba51ccc03002300856d8c7
90bac9e2114b8daa29b1bf2ed56b6f292ff21bb993392ac2877a9a0fc4455900
3fca87b5c31c1d2c3caceaaaabebe4eb26ccf3141e7ec045721ad9b72aa0b54a
e569af8fd8d7f1273edd1c35d988ac87e71d1c63953c594606497b0eb2e64ebe
```
